Although persecution may cause us to leave our work, it can also send us to work elsewhere. Wherever a believer is driven, he shares the knowledge of the gospel and demonstrates the preciousness of Christ in every place. When a simple desire to do good influences someone's heart, it will be difficult to prevent them from finding opportunities to be useful.

As the gospel spreads, evil spirits are displaced, especially unclean spirits. All inclinations toward fleshly desires that war against the soul are like these. Illnesses are mentioned here—the most challenging to cure by natural means and the clearest signs of sin's disease. Pride, ambition, and a desire for grandeur have always caused great harm, both in the world and within the church. People said of Simon, “This man is the great power of God.” See how ignorant and thoughtless people mistake! But how powerful is Divine grace, by which they were brought to Christ, who is Truth itself! The people not only listened to what Philip said but were fully convinced that it came from God, not from humans, and submitted themselves to be guided by it. Even bad people, and those whose hearts still crave wealth, may come before God as his people do, and for a time remain with them. Many wonder at the proofs of Divine truths who have never experienced their power. The gospel preached can have a common effect on a person’s soul without producing inward holiness. Not everyone who professes to believe the gospel is truly converted.

The Holy Spirit had not yet fallen upon any of these people in the extraordinary ways demonstrated by the descent of the Spirit on the day of Pentecost. We may find encouragement in this example when we pray to God to give the renewing graces of the Holy Spirit to all for whose spiritual welfare we are concerned, because that includes all blessings. No one can give the Holy Spirit by laying hands on someone; but we should use our best efforts to instruct those for whom we pray. Simon Magus was ambitious to have the honor of being an apostle, but he did not care about having the spirit and character of a Christian. He desired to gain honor for himself more than to do good to others. Peter shows him his error. He considered worldly wealth enough to secure what relates to eternal life, to purchase forgiveness of sin, the gift of the Holy Spirit, and eternal life. This was such an erroneous belief that it could not coexist with a state of grace. Our hearts are as they are in God’s sight, who cannot be deceived. And if they are not right in his sight, our religion is worthless and will fail us. A proud and covetous heart cannot be right with God. It is possible for someone to remain under the power of sin while putting on a form of godliness. When tempted with money to do evil, recognize how insignificant money is, and reject it. Do not think that Christianity is a way to make a living in this world. There is much wickedness in the heart’s thoughts, false notions, corrupt affections, and wicked plans, which must be repented of, or we will be ruined. But they will be forgiven when we repent. The doubt here concerns the sincerity of Simon's repentance, not his pardon if his repentance was sincere. Grant us, Lord, a faith different from that which made Simon wonder only and did not sanctify his heart. May we abhor all thoughts of making religion serve pride or ambition. And keep us from that subtle poison of spiritual pride, which seeks glory for itself even in humility. May we seek only the honor that comes from God.

Philip was directed to go to a desert place. Sometimes God opens doors of opportunity to his ministers in unexpected places. We should strive to do good to those we encounter while traveling. We should not be as shy of strangers as some are. As for people about whom we know nothing else, we know that they have souls. It is wise for businesspeople to redeem time for holy duties; to fill every minute with something that will prove beneficial. When reading the word of God, we should often pause to consider who and what the sacred writers were speaking about; but especially our thoughts should be focused on the Redeemer. The Ethiopian was convinced by the teaching of the Holy Spirit that Scripture was being fulfilled exactly as prophesied, understood the nature of Messiah’s kingdom and salvation, and desired to be numbered among Christ's disciples. Those who seek truth and spend their time searching the Scriptures will surely benefit. The Ethiopian’s declaration should be understood as expressing simple reliance on Christ for salvation and complete devotion to Him. Let us not be satisfied until we receive faith, as the Ethiopian did, through diligent study of the Holy Scriptures and the teaching of the Spirit of God; let us not be satisfied until it is established as a principle in our hearts. As soon as he was baptized, the Spirit of God carried Philip away so that he no longer saw him; but this confirmed the Ethiopian’s faith. When someone seeking salvation becomes acquainted with Jesus and his gospel, they will go on their way rejoicing and will fulfill their role in society and perform their duties differently than before. Although baptized in the name of the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit with water, it is not enough without the baptism of the Holy Spirit. Lord, grant this to each of us; then we shall go on our way rejoicing.
